behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-to-text model output quality (speaker diarization/label retention), which mocks can hide. Workflow was considered and rejected because this behavior targets a single model-step artifact (STT output), not a required sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the actual STT provider and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for known utterances; fail if labels are missing, collapsed, or swapped.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic and can fail within one local function boundary without real I/O or model judgment. Integration was considered and rejected because the behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff beyond the parser transform itself.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript text snippets to the parsing function and assert exact extraction of assignee and task text into the expected input schema, including malformed/edge phrasing cases.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM content decisions (coverage/completeness of generated action items per assignee), which deterministic stubs would mask. Workflow was considered and rejected because the core assertion is on one generation output quality decision, not a required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcripts with known task assignments to the production summarization model and verify each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is a module-coordination failure boundary: identified-decision outputs must be handed off and mapped into the correct schema section. System/Agentic were considered and rejected because the behavior explicitly starts from already identified decisions and tests routing/mapping, not real OS/network behavior or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire extractor output module to schema-assembler module in-process (no real APIs) and assert identified decisions land in `decisions` while narrative fields exclude them.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether the model actually surfaces and classifies decision content correctly from transcript text; mocked outputs would always pass and hide real misses. Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior text centers on transcript-to-summary decision placement, not an explicit multi-step agent pipeline requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing known decision statements through the production summarizer and assert those decisions appear in `decisions` with acceptable recall, and are not only present in freeform narrative.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This requires multiple sequential agentic steps (segment-level transcription/summarization plus merge) where handoff and accumulated model outputs determine final correctness. Lower levels miss failures in cross-step ordering, context carryover, and merge coherence across model calls.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real long-audio path (>60 min) end-to-end with actual segmentation and model calls, then assert segment ordering is preserved, all segments are included, and merged summary remains coherent and structurally valid.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: By definition this is a full multi-stage pipeline with real transcription and summarization model steps plus delivery orchestration, so real failures emerge across chained agentic boundaries. Any lower-level isolation would miss pipeline-level breakages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end job on a real meeting recording from upload through final structured output delivery; assert completion status, schema validity, and absence of manual retries/intervention.
